name: Berganzo Water Trail
name_original: Ruta del agua del río Inglares— circular Berganzo
description: Hike the moderate 5.3-mile Water route of the Inglares river - circular Berganzo, featuring waterfalls and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Zambrana offers diverse landscapes within Spain's Basque Country, characterized by varied terrain including river valleys, mountain peaks, and historical sites. The region features well-maintained trails that traverse natural monuments and provide panoramic views. Hikers can explore areas with significant elevation changes as well as more accessible paths along waterways. This blend of natural beauty and cultural elements makes Zambrana a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Zambrana The most popular hiking route is Berganzo Water Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Inglares River, featuring picturesque waterfalls and river views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Txulato, Peña Infinito y Castillo de Portilla — Southern Sierras of Álava, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This trail offers significant elevation gain and leads to historical sites like Portilla Castle, providing expansive views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the Monte Cruz de Motrico — circular desde Ircio, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a mountain peak, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Zambrana is defined by river routes, mountain peaks, and historical elements. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more demanding routes. The routes in Zambrana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 400 reviews. More than 3,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Zambrana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Zambrana?
A: Zambrana offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 260 distinct trails available for exploration. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Zambrana?
A: Yes, Zambrana has several trails suitable for families. The region features easily navigable paths, particularly along river routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035888095/berganzo-water-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berganzo Water Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular route that is accessible to various fitness levels, making it a great choice for a family outing.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Zambrana?
A: Many trails in Zambrana are generally accessible for dogs, especially those in natural areas. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions before you go.

Q: Are there trails with waterfalls in Zambrana?
A: Absolutely! The area is known for its beautiful river routes and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035888095/berganzo-water-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berganzo Water Trail&lt;/a&gt; along the Inglares River is a prime example, featuring picturesque waterfalls. You can also explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5474808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ollerías Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6864843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Inglares Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Zambrana's hiking routes often integrate historical and cultural elements. You can encounter sites like the restored &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3223361" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portilla Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers stunning views, especially on trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050795809/txulato-pena-infinito-y-castillo-de-portilla-southern-sierras-of-alava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txulato, Peña Infinito y Castillo de Portilla&lt;/a&gt;. The village of Berganzo also features the Romanesque parish church of San Miguel, adding cultural interest to your hike.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Zambrana offers several circular hiking routes, which are great for exploring without retracing your steps. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035888095/berganzo-water-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berganzo Water Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.3-mile loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1297159729/monte-cruz-de-motrico-circular-desde-ircio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Cruz de Motrico — circular desde Ircio&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through varied terrain to a mountain peak.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Zambrana?
A: Zambrana's diverse landscapes make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for exploring river routes and mountain peaks. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially on more exposed trails. Winter hiking is also possible, though some higher elevation routes might require appropriate gear.

Q: How difficult are the hiking trails in Zambrana?
A: The hiking trails in Zambrana cater to a wide range of abilities. You'll find everything from easy, accessible paths suitable for casual walkers to more demanding routes with significant elevation gain that require good fitness and sure-footedness. Out of nearly 270 trails, about 63 are easy, 174 are moderate, and 31 are considered difficult.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging hikes in the area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Zambrana offers several longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042919643/cruz-de-motrico-pena-la-abeja-and-pena-escalera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Motrico, Peña la Abeja, and Peña Escalera Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.4-mile trail with substantial elevation changes, providing a rewarding full-day adventure through mountain peaks.

Q: What do other hikers say about Zambrana's trails?
A: The trails in Zambrana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's varied terrain, the beauty of the river routes and waterfalls, and the stunning panoramic views from mountain peaks and historical sites like Portilla Castle.

Q: What are the natural features I can expect to see while hiking?
A: Zambrana's natural beauty is characterized by diverse features. You'll encounter picturesque river valleys, prominent mountain peaks offering expansive vistas, and charming waterfalls along waterways. The terrain varies from lush forested areas to more open, rocky landscapes, providing a rich and engaging hiking experience.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Zambrana has a good selection of easy trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. Many of the river routes, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5342484" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Water Route – Agua Jugalez-Inglares Route loop from Berganzo&lt;/a&gt;, offer gentle gradients and well-maintained paths, allowing for an enjoyable and accessible outdoor experience.
tips: The town is located in the Sierra de Cantabria, on the northern slope of Mount Toloño and is crossed by the Inglares River, which divides it into two neighbourhoods: Arriba and Abajo, linked by two simple bridges, one of them, called Puente de Arriba, locally called Puente de Palo.

Simple and spectacular walk following the course of the river. It is worth going to go to the shore on the trails that start from the general path. Especially recommended in autumn and with enough water in the riverbed.
Nice waterfall among thick vegetation, located at the end of the path known as the "Ruta del Agua"

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Water route of the Inglares river - circular Berganzo?
faqs.answer: The full circular route usually takes between 2.5 to 3 hours to complete. This estimate can vary depending on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the scenic spots along the way.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is generally considered to be of easy to moderate difficulty, making it a light, charming, and family-friendly option. While there are a couple of short, steeper sections, it's suitable for most skill levels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Water route of the Inglares river?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, primarily following the Inglares River. You'll walk through forested areas, alongside an old water channel, and encounter numerous waterfalls and river formations. The path includes natural, unpaved sections, as well as some paved and asphalt parts.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty. Key highlights include the many cascades and rapids of the Inglares River, especially the captivating Ollerías Waterfall (also known as Las Herrerías waterfall). You'll also enjoy views of the valley and the silhouette of the Castle of Ocio or Lanos from sections alongside the old channel. The trail starts and ends in the historic Berganzo Village itself.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but it's particularly enjoyable in autumn when the Inglares River has ample flow, enhancing the waterfalls and rapids. It's also popular in summer and on weekends, though it can get busy.

faqs.question: Is the Water route of the Inglares river - circular Berganzo dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is family-friendly and generally accessible, specific rules regarding dogs are not explicitly stated. It's usually advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of a permit or entrance fee for hiking the trail itself. However, due to its popularity, advance reservation is required for parking, especially on weekends and during peak seasons, to manage visitor numbers and ensure sustainability.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the trail, and how do I access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Berganzo Village. Parking is available, but it's crucial to note that advance reservation for parking is required, particularly on weekends and during busy periods. This helps manage visitor flow.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter along the Inglares River?
faqs.answer: The river and its surrounding environment provide a habitat for various species. Keep an eye out for European otters and minks, which are known to inhabit this area.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with other official trails. The entire path is part of the "Uraren bidea" trail, and it also covers significant sections of the "Ibilbide historikoa: Araba" trail. Smaller portions intersect with "Vuelta a los Payos" and "Ruta de los Castillos".

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near Berganzo?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the heart of Berganzo Village, which is a historic settlement. While specific establishments aren't detailed, you can typically find local amenities in such villages. It's recommended to check local listings for current options for food and lodging.
